title: Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani
description: Experience the beloved classic Murgh Makhani, a genuine Indian masterpiece featuring tender chicken enveloped in a rich, creamy, and subtly sweet tomato-based gravy. This dish offers a harmonious blend of smoky flavors from the marinated chicken and the luxurious richness of butter and cream, making it a truly comforting and iconic meal.

ingredients.item: Boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breast

ingredients.notes: from ripe, blanched and pureed tomatoes
soaked in warm water for 15 minutes

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with plain yogurt, 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste, 1 tsp Kashmiri red chili powder, 1/2 tsp turmeric powder, 1/2 tsp garam masala, 1/2 tsp salt, and lemon juice. Mix well to ensure the chicken is evenly coated. Cover the bowl and refrigerate for at least 1 hour, preferably 2-4 hours, or overnight for best flavor.

instructions.title: Cook the Marinated Chicken
instructions.text: Heat 1 tbsp butter and 1 tbsp oil in a heavy-bottomed pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Once hot, add the marinated chicken pieces in a single layer, ensuring not to overcrowd the pan. Cook the chicken for 5-7 minutes on each side, until lightly charred and cooked through. Remove the chicken from the pan and set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare the Gravy Base
instructions.text: In the same pan, add the remaining 1 tbsp butter. Once melted, add the finely chopped onion and sauté until translucent and lightly golden, about 5-7 minutes. Add the remaining 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste and green chilies, cooking for another minute until fragrant. Stir in the tomato puree, 1/2 tsp Kashmiri red chili powder, and 1/4 tsp turmeric powder. Cook, stirring occasionally, until the mixture thickens and the oil starts to separate from the sides, about 8-10 minutes.

instructions.title: Simmer and Blend the Gravy
instructions.text: Drain the soaked cashews and add them to the tomato mixture in the pan. Pour in 1/2 cup of water or chicken stock. Bring to a gentle simmer, then cover and cook for 10 minutes to allow the flavors to meld and cashews to soften further. Remove from heat and let it cool slightly. Carefully transfer the mixture to a blender and blend until completely smooth. For an extra velvety texture, pass the blended gravy through a fine-mesh sieve.

instructions.title: Finish the Gravy
instructions.text: Return the smooth gravy to the pan. Add 1 tsp garam masala, 1 tsp crushed kasuri methi, 1/2 tsp sugar or honey, and salt to taste. Bring the gravy to a gentle simmer over low heat, stirring continuously to prevent scorching. Let it cook for 5 minutes, allowing the spices to infuse deeply into the sauce.

instructions.title: Combine Chicken and Serve
instructions.text: Add the cooked chicken pieces to the simmering gravy and stir to coat them evenly. Pour in the heavy cream and the last 1 tbsp of unsalted butter. Stir gently until the butter has melted and the cream is fully incorporated. Heat through for another 2-3 minutes, ensuring the chicken is warmed through but not overcooked. Garnish generously with fresh chopped coriander leaves and serve hot.

tips: For the best texture, marinate the chicken for at least 2 hours. Overnight marination in the refrigerator yields even more tender and flavorful results.
Using Kashmiri red chili powder is crucial for achieving the characteristic vibrant red color of Butter Chicken without excessive heat. Adjust regular red chili powder if substituting.
Do not skip blending and optionally straining the gravy. This step is key to achieving the silky-smooth, restaurant-quality texture that Murgh Makhani is known for.
Always add the cream and final butter at the very end of cooking, and heat gently to prevent splitting and retain their rich flavor and texture.
article:
## History of Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani

Butter Chicken, or Murgh Makhani, holds a storied place in Indian culinary history, tracing its origins to the Moti Mahal restaurant in Delhi in the 1950s. The legend attributes its creation to Kundan Lal Gujral, who also famously invented Dal Makhani and Tandoori Chicken. Faced with leftover tandoori chicken, he devised a rich tomato-based gravy, enriched with butter and cream, to keep the chicken moist and flavorful. This innovative dish quickly captured the hearts of diners, including India's first Prime Minister, Jawaharlal Nehru, and became a global sensation, embodying the richness and warmth of Indian hospitality. It remains a testament to ingenious culinary improvisation.

## Why This Recipe Works

This recipe for Butter Chicken works by leveraging a synergy of authentic Indian techniques and flavor profiles. The initial marination of the chicken in yogurt and spices tenderizes the meat and infuses it with deep flavor, while pan-frying creates a slight char, mimicking the smoky notes of a tandoor. The gravy's foundation of ripe tomatoes and cashews provides a natural sweetness, tang, and body, which is then balanced with aromatic spices like garam masala and kasuri methi. The generous addition of butter and fresh cream at the end not only imparts a luxurious, silky texture but also rounds out the flavors, creating a dish that is both complex and incredibly comforting.

## Chef Tips for Perfection

To elevate your Butter Chicken, begin with high-quality boneless chicken thighs for maximum tenderness and flavor. When preparing the gravy, ensure you cook the tomato base thoroughly until the oil separates; this deepens the flavor and removes any raw tomato taste. Blending the gravy until absolutely smooth is non-negotiable for that signature velvety texture – consider straining it for extra refinement. Always crush kasuri methi between your palms before adding it to release its full, distinctive aroma. Lastly, resist the urge to boil the gravy vigorously after adding cream; a gentle simmer will maintain its richness and prevent it from splitting.

## Serving Suggestions

Butter Chicken is a versatile dish that pairs wonderfully with a variety of Indian breads and rice. For a truly authentic experience, serve it with warm, fluffy Naan bread (plain, garlic, or butter naan) or Tandoori Roti, perfect for scooping up every last drop of the luscious gravy. Basmati rice, either plain steamed or a fragrant Jeera Rice (cumin rice), also makes an excellent accompaniment, soaking up the rich sauce beautifully. Complete your meal with a side of cooling Raita (yogurt dip) or a simple kachumber salad (diced cucumber, tomato, onion) to add a refreshing counterpoint to the richness of the Murgh Makhani.

jsonLd.name: Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani
jsonLd.description: Experience the beloved classic Murgh Makhani, a genuine Indian masterpiece featuring tender chicken enveloped in a rich, creamy, and subtly sweet tomato-based gravy. This dish offers a harmonious blend of smoky flavors from the marinated chicken and the luxurious richness of butter and cream, making it a truly comforting and iconic meal.
jsonLd.keywords: Indian, Indian, authentic, traditional, chicken, Indian, authentic, traditional, chicken, makhani, creamy, curry
recipeIngredient: 500g Boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breast (cut into 1-inch pieces)
1 tsp Kashmiri red chili powder (for color and mild heat)
1.5 cups Tomato puree (from ripe, blanched and pureed tomatoes)
1/4 cup Cashews (soaked in warm water for 15 minutes)
1 tsp Kasuri Methi (dried fenugreek leaves) (crushed between palms)
1/2 tsp Sugar or honey (to balance acidity)
2 tbsp Fresh coriander leaves (chopped, for garnish)

recipeInstructions.name: Marinate the Chicken
recipeInstructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with plain yogurt, 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste, 1 tsp Kashmiri red chili powder, 1/2 tsp turmeric powder, 1/2 tsp garam masala, 1/2 tsp salt, and lemon juice. Mix well to ensure the chicken is evenly coated. Cover the bowl and refrigerate for at least 1 hour, preferably 2-4 hours, or overnight for best flavor.

recipeInstructions.name: Cook the Marinated Chicken
recipeInstructions.text: Heat 1 tbsp butter and 1 tbsp oil in a heavy-bottomed pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Once hot, add the marinated chicken pieces in a single layer, ensuring not to overcrowd the pan. Cook the chicken for 5-7 minutes on each side, until lightly charred and cooked through. Remove the chicken from the pan and set aside.

recipeInstructions.name: Prepare the Gravy Base
recipeInstructions.text: In the same pan, add the remaining 1 tbsp butter. Once melted, add the finely chopped onion and sauté until translucent and lightly golden, about 5-7 minutes. Add the remaining 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste and green chilies, cooking for another minute until fragrant. Stir in the tomato puree, 1/2 tsp Kashmiri red chili powder, and 1/4 tsp turmeric powder. Cook, stirring occasionally, until the mixture thickens and the oil starts to separate from the sides, about 8-10 minutes.

recipeInstructions.name: Simmer and Blend the Gravy
recipeInstructions.text: Drain the soaked cashews and add them to the tomato mixture in the pan. Pour in 1/2 cup of water or chicken stock. Bring to a gentle simmer, then cover and cook for 10 minutes to allow the flavors to meld and cashews to soften further. Remove from heat and let it cool slightly. Carefully transfer the mixture to a blender and blend until completely smooth. For an extra velvety texture, pass the blended gravy through a fine-mesh sieve.

recipeInstructions.name: Finish the Gravy
recipeInstructions.text: Return the smooth gravy to the pan. Add 1 tsp garam masala, 1 tsp crushed kasuri methi, 1/2 tsp sugar or honey, and salt to taste. Bring the gravy to a gentle simmer over low heat, stirring continuously to prevent scorching. Let it cook for 5 minutes, allowing the spices to infuse deeply into the sauce.

recipeInstructions.name: Combine Chicken and Serve
recipeInstructions.text: Add the cooked chicken pieces to the simmering gravy and stir to coat them evenly. Pour in the heavy cream and the last 1 tbsp of unsalted butter. Stir gently until the butter has melted and the cream is fully incorporated. Heat through for another 2-3 minutes, ensuring the chicken is warmed through but not overcooked. Garnish generously with fresh chopped coriander leaves and serve hot.
